title: Define a Role's tasks/main.yml
description: Write the tasks/main.yml for a 'webserver' role that installs nginx, enables it, and copies an index file.
starterCode:
---
# This is roles/webserver/tasks/main.yml
# Write tasks to:
# 1. Install nginx (ansible.builtin.package, state: present)
# 2. Start and enable nginx service (ansible.builtin.service, state: started, enabled: true)
# 3. Copy files/index.html to /var/www/html/index.html
#    Use the 'owner' parameter to set owner to 'www-data'
solutionCode:
---
- name: Install nginx
  ansible.builtin.package:
    name: nginx
    state: present

- name: Start and enable nginx
  ansible.builtin.service:
    name: nginx
    state: started
    enabled: true

- name: Copy index page
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    src: index.html
    dest: /var/www/html/index.html
    owner: www-data
    mode: '0644'
hints: In a role's tasks/main.yml, you list tasks directly (no play header needed)
ansible.builtin.service 'enabled: true' ensures nginx starts on boot
In a role, 'src: index.html' looks in the role's files/ directory automatically

validationRules.errorMessage: Enable nginx to start on boot with 'enabled: true'
Use ansible.builtin.copy to deploy the index file

simulatedOutput.success:
Role task validation passed!

Tasks found:

  [ok] Start and enable nginx (ansible.builtin.service)
  [ok] Copy index page (ansible.builtin.copy)

Role structure is valid.
